```text
○ lead-33r [BUG] · request_bugfix: shopsystem-templates — canonical settings.json hooks must not embed shop identity   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: bug
Created: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
The canonical .claude/settings.json templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json must not require shop-identity substitution. Today the BC variant ships 'shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}'; bootstrap pours it byte-for-byte (cli.py:423), the placeholder is never substituted, and the Claude Code harness executes the literal command — producing 'shop name {{SHOP_NAME}} is not registered'. This bug is currently latent for every BC that hasn't had a Claude Code session opened in its own repo dir; surfaced when starting shopsystem-docs. After B-MESSAGING (implicit CWD resolution) lands, the canonical hook becomes bare 'shop-msg prime' for both shop types. Scenarios to author: (1) canonical bc.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' with no addressing flags and no placeholders; (2) canonical lead.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' (currently 'bd prime' — verify whether bd prime stays alongside or is replaced; PO judgment); (3) a poured BC's .claude/settings.json contains no {{...}} placeholders; (4) repour via 'shop-templates update' replaces the old hook content. Discriminator: capability exists (canonical bootstrap pours settings.json) but the hook-content tightening is unpinned. Vehicle: request_bugfix. Anchor scenarios that need re-examination: 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d (byte-for-byte pour — these remain correct; the new scenarios sit alongside them, pinning the body). Depends on: B1 (shop-msg implicit lookup must exist first).

NOTES
Dispatched 2026-05-27 via 'shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-33r' carrying 5 scenarios (features/templates/100-104 bodies, stripped of Feature line; wrapped feature title 'Canonical .claude/settings.json hooks must not embed shop identity (PDR-009)'). Wire hashes after CLI canonicalization+wrapping: scenario 100 -> 10828c65d2abbc8b; scenario 101 -> 6356da315e77d5b3; scenario 102 -> 65610830de660ac5; scenario 103 -> 5cad879228477481; scenario 104 -> f40fa77a3a04ffca. Empirical pre-state cited in dispatch description: read repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/bc.json (ships 'shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}' inner-hook command — literal placeholder survives byte-for-byte pour per scenarios 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d) and lead.json (ships 'bd prime' as sole SessionStart command — missing 'shop-msg prime' alongside per PDR-009). BC @scenario_hash enumeration via grep -r identified anchor scenarios 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d (NOT retired) and lead-side 'sole bd prime' hash 71797e9017c95fed (Implementer instructed in dispatch description to retire this when landing scenarios 101/102). Lead-side scenarios 75/96 hashes b730039138e11246 / c8bce31c4af1cff4 not on BC pinned set; no BC-side action needed for those. Verified via shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-33r — all 5 scenarios present with @bc:shopsystem-templates tag. Dependency chain confirmed via bd show lead-33r: blocks lead-0u2 / lead-yhr / lead-u16 / lead-8yl (the 4 re-pour beads) — those remain blocked pending lead-33r work_done.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ lead-i18: assign_scenarios: shopsystem-messaging — shop-msg implicit CWD-based shop resolution ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ lead-0u2: request_maintenance: shopsystem-bc-launcher — re-pour .claude/settings.json after canonical fix ● P2
  ← ○ lead-8yl: request_maintenance: shopsystem-devcontainer — re-pour .claude/settings.json after canonical fix ● P2
  ← ○ lead-u16: request_maintenance: shopsystem-docs — re-pour .claude/settings.json after canonical fix ● P2
  ← ○ lead-yhr: request_maintenance: shopsystem-templates — re-pour own .claude/settings.json after canonical fix ● P2
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./features/templates/100-canonical-claude-settings-json-hook-commands-contain-no-placeholder-substrings.gherkin`

```text
1	Feature: Canonical ".claude/settings.json" hook commands contain no "{{...}}" placeholder substrings
2	
3	  Per PDR-003 alt F (scenarios 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d), bootstrap pours ".claude/*" files byte-for-byte from canonical package data with no substitution. A canonical hook command containing a "{{...}}" placeholder substring therefore survives the pour as a literal "{{...}}" sequence in the consumer's ".claude/settings.json", and the Claude Code harness then executes the literal sequence — silently producing a wrong-shop or unrecognized-name error. The canonical template surface must therefore commit, at the package-data level, that no hook command body contains placeholder-shaped content.
4	
5	  Per PDR-009 (decided 2026-05-27), shop identity is never embedded in canonical content; "shop-msg" resolves the invoking shop's identity from CWD by walking up looking for ".claude/shop/name.md" and ".claude/shop/type.md". This eliminates any architectural reason for a canonical hook command to embed shop identity via placeholder substitution.
6	
7	  Scenario Outline: every inner-hook "command" string in the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for "<shop_type>" contains no occurrence of the substring "{{" and no occurrence of the substring "}}"
8	    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
9	    And the returned body is parsed as JSON
10	    Then for every top-level hook-event key under "hooks" (including but not limited to "SessionStart" and "PreCompact"), and for every element of that hook-event's array, and for every entry of that element's inner "hooks" array, the entry's "command" string contains no occurrence of the substring "{{" and no occurrence of the substring "}}"
11	
12	    Examples:
13	      | shop_type |
14	      | bc        |
15	      | lead      |
16
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	Feature: Canonical ".claude/settings.json" "SessionStart" includes bare "shop-msg prime" with no addressing flags
2	
3	  Supersedes the "sole inner-hook command" property of scenario 75 (features/templates/75-canonical-lead-claude-settings-json-ships-with-bd-prime-as-sole-sessionstart-hook.gherkin) and scenario 96 (features/templates/96-canonical-bc-claude-settings-json-ships-with-bd-prime-as-sole-sessionstart-hook.gherkin). Those scenarios pinned "bd prime" as the SOLE SessionStart command on the premise that no other short-lived, cleanly-terminating SessionStart hook applied. Per PDR-009, "shop-msg prime" is a second short-lived, cleanly-terminating SessionStart hook that DOES apply (it checks the messaging-DSN reachability and reports pending inbox count — the orientation that lets the router know whether the messaging surface is alive before user work begins). Both hooks now belong; the "sole" qualifier is retired. The Implementer must locate the BC-side hashes of scenarios 75 and 96 in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/ via the grep mechanism pinned by scenarios 92-95 and retire the "sole" pin when landing these new scenarios.
4	
5	  PO judgment (PDR-009): the canonical hook command is bare "shop-msg prime" — no "--bc" or "--lead" addressing flag, no shop-name placeholder. "shop-msg" resolves the invoking shop's identity from CWD via the walk-up mechanism PDR-009 commits. Canonical content carries no shop identity. The "bare" property is essential — embedding the name (via placeholder or pour-time substitution) would re-introduce the very bug class PDR-009 exists to close.
6	
7	  Scenario Outline: exactly one inner-hook "command" entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in the canonical ".claude/settings.json" for "<shop_type>" equals the literal string "shop-msg prime"
8	    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
9	    And the returned body is parsed as JSON
10	    Then the parsed value at "hooks.SessionStart" is a JSON array of length at least 1
11	    And exactly one inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string equal to the literal value "shop-msg prime"
12	    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string that starts with "shop-msg prime " followed by additional characters
13	    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--bc"
14	    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--lead"
15	    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--bc-root"
16	    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--lead-root"
17	
18	    Examples:
19	      | shop_type |
20	      | bc        |
21	      | lead      |
22

Those scenarios pinned "bd prime" as the SOLE SessionStart command on the premise that no other short-lived, cleanly-terminating SessionStart hook applied. Per PDR-009, "shop-msg prime" is a second short-lived, cleanly-terminating SessionStart hook that DOES apply (it checks the messaging-DSN reachability and reports pending inbox count — the orientation that lets the router know whether the messaging surface is alive before user work begins). Both hooks now belong; the "sole" qualifier is retired. The Implementer must locate the BC-side hashes of scenarios 75 and 96 in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/ via the grep mechanism pinned by scenarios 92-95 and retire the "sole" pin when landing these new scenarios.

  PO judgment (PDR-009): the canonical hook command is bare "shop-msg prime" — no "--bc" or "--lead" addressing flag, no shop-name placeholder. "shop-msg" resolves the invoking shop's identity from CWD via the walk-up mechanism PDR-009 commits. Canonical content carries no shop identity. The "bare" property is essential — embedding the name (via placeholder or pour-time substitution) would re-introduce the very bug class PDR-009 exists to close.

  Scenario Outline: exactly one inner-hook "command" entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in the canonical ".claude/settings.json" for "<shop_type>" equals the literal string "shop-msg prime"
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
    And the returned body is parsed as JSON
    Then the parsed value at "hooks.SessionStart" is a JSON array of length at least 1
    And exactly one inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string equal to the literal value "shop-msg prime"
    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string that starts with "shop-msg prime " followed by additional characters
    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--bc"
    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--lead"
    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--bc-root"
    And no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" has a "command" string containing the substring "--lead-root"

    Examples:
      | shop_type |
      | bc        |
      | lead      |

  This scenario is the bootstrap-side companion of scenario 100 (canonical-side guard). Scenario 100 pins that canonical package data carries no placeholder substrings; this scenario pins the same property at the consumer end — after "shop-templates init" or "shop-templates update" pours ".claude/settings.json" into a target shop, the resulting file on disk must also be free of "{{...}}" substrings. Together the two scenarios make the absence of placeholder substitution observable both from the publisher side and the consumer side, eliminating the regression class where canonical and poured copies could disagree on the placeholder property.

  Scenario Outline: after "shop-templates init" or "shop-templates update" pours ".claude/settings.json" into a target shop of type "<shop_type>", the poured file contains no occurrence of the substring "{{" and no occurrence of the substring "}}"
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" "<entry_point>" against target "<target>" with shop type "<shop_type>" and shop name "<shop_name>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And the file at "<target>/.claude/settings.json" exists
    And the contents of "<target>/.claude/settings.json" contain no occurrence of the substring "{{"
    And the contents of "<target>/.claude/settings.json" contain no occurrence of the substring "}}"

    Examples:
      | entry_point | shop_type | shop_name              | target                     |
      | init        | bc        | shopsystem-docs        | /tmp/example-bc-shop       |
      | init        | lead      | shopsystem-product     | /tmp/example-lead-shop     |
      | update      | bc        | shopsystem-docs        | /tmp/example-bc-shop       |
      | update      | lead      | shopsystem-product     | /tmp/example-lead-shop     |

```

  Scenario 61 (features/templates/61-update-re-pours-claude-settings-json-as-managed-file.gherkin) already pins that "shop-templates update" re-pours ".claude/settings.json" byte-for-byte from the current canonical, treating it as bootstrap-managed. This scenario is the targeted regression guard for the specific failure mode that motivated lead-33r: a shop that was bootstrapped with an OLD canonical "settings.json" carrying a stale placeholder-bearing hook command (e.g., "shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}") must, after "shop-templates update", carry the NEW canonical hook command ("shop-msg prime" with no placeholder and no addressing flag). The scenario fixes the inner-hook command string as the observable property so the regression cannot pass scenario 61's generic "equals canonical byte-for-byte" without ALSO passing the targeted placeholder-removal property.

  Scenario Outline: "shop-templates update" against a target shop whose ".claude/settings.json" carries a stale placeholder-bearing hook command replaces that command with the current canonical bare "shop-msg prime"
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
    And the file at "<target>/.claude/settings.json" contains an inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" whose "command" string is the stale literal "shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against target "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And after the invocation no inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in "<target>/.claude/settings.json" has a "command" string containing the substring "{{SHOP_NAME}}"
    And after the invocation exactly one inner-hook entry under "hooks.SessionStart" in "<target>/.claude/settings.json" has a "command" string equal to the literal value "shop-msg prime"
    And after the invocation "<target>/.claude/settings.json" equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name              | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-docs        | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product     | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

Owner: David Stenglein · Assignee: David Stenglein · Type: task

DESCRIPTION
Sibling to lead-6nt (registry surface). Today's Monitor event lead-dual-1 work_done from shopsystem-messaging revealed that BDD step defs in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ invoke shop-msg respond using the canonical bc=shopsystem-messaging name (not the per-test tmp_path namespace), so the respond payload writes to the production lead inbox and broadcasts via LISTEN/NOTIFY.

All map to feature files in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ (consume_outbox, cwd_implicit_shop_resolution, pending_and_read_inbox, respond_*, send_request_maintenance*, watch).

WHY NOT FOLDED INTO LEAD-6NT

Critically, simply rewriting step defs to use tmp_path-namespaced bc args is NOT a valid fix: scenarios 25, 38, and others EXPLICITLY assert lead-inbox routing behavior with canonical names — that routing IS the contract under test.

✓ lead-6nt · mechanism observation (lead-i18): test fixtures register production canonical names without save/restore   [● P1 · CLOSED]
Owner: David Stenglein · Assignee: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-27 · Started: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
BC shopsystem-messaging flagged in lead-i18 work_done summary that the test-suite step helper _register_shop (tests/conftest.py:3975) calls registry_add() against the postgres shop_registry using production canonical names (e.g., 'shopsystem-docs', 'unregistered-bc', 'shopsystem-product') derived from feature-file Given steps. The session-scoped fixture only saves/restores 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product'; it does NOT save/restore 'shopsystem-docs' or any other production BC name a future scenario may use.

LOAD-BEARING: The cwd_implicit_shop_resolution feature (just landed via lead-i18) registers 'shopsystem-docs' pointing at a tmp_path during the test run. If the test session crashes between registry_add and registry_remove, or if the cleanup path is bypassed, the production registry row for 'shopsystem-docs' is left pointing at a defunct pytest tmp_path. CWD-implicit shop-msg invocations from the real repos/shopsystem-docs/ working tree would then resolve via the corrupted registry entry. The mechanism (CWD-implicit resolution) is the very thing whose hygiene this fixture pattern threatens.

VERIFICATION ANCHORS (in repos/shopsystem-messaging):
- tests/conftest.py:3975 _register_shop -- direct registry_add, no save
- tests/conftest.py:157-174 _ensure_session_lead -- only 'shopsystem-product' / 'shopsystem product' saved
- features/cwd_implicit_shop_resolution.feature lines 6, 16, 54, 75 -- production canonical names appearing in Given steps

RECOMMENDED FOLLOW-UP (defer dispatch):
1. Extend _SAVED_PRODUCTION_ENTRIES initialization to cover every canonical name that step defs may pass to _register_shop, OR
2. Refactor _register_shop to use disambiguated test-only names (uuid suffix) when the scenario does not specifically require a production name, OR
3. Add a per-test cleanup (autouse function-scoped fixture) that snapshots & restores every name _register_shop touches during that test.

Likely vehicle: request_maintenance (flat refactor of test hygiene, no new scenarios) to shopsystem-messaging. Defer until either (a) a related scenario lands that would amplify the risk, or (b) an actual corruption event occurs.

ANCHORS:
- Source: lead-i18 work_done summary (consumed via shop-msg read outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-i18)
- Related: lead-tsj (postgres state-loss event) -- same registry, different failure mode
- Architecture: PDR-009 (CWD-implicit resolution; the resolution mechanism whose hygiene is at risk)

○ lead-tsj · mechanism observation: postgres-backed shop-msg state lost across LISTEN connection drop   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
Mechanism observation logged 2026-05-27.

SYMPTOM
'shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product' (in-session Monitor watcher armed
at session start) died with:

  psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly
      This probably means the server terminated abnormally
      before or while processing the request.

The last event streamed before the drop was 'lead-eqx work_done'. On
operator-initiated restart of the watcher the CLI surfaced the next layer
of damage: the messaging registry was empty (7 expected entries: 2 lead
aliases — 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product' — plus 5 BCs:
shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-bc-launcher,
shopsystem-devcontainer, shopsystem-docs). Inbox and outbox state were
also lost: 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead "shopsystem product"' and
'shop-msg pending outbox --lead "shopsystem product"' both returned
empty, including in-flight rows that had not yet been consumed
(lead-i18 assign_scenarios and lead-bhp request_bugfix, both addressed
to shopsystem-messaging, both verified deposited earlier in the same
session via 'shop-msg read inbox').

The 7 entries were manually re-registered and the two in-flight
dispatches were re-emitted (see lead-i18 and lead-bhp notes for
wire-hash equivalence to originals).

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSE
Postgres instance backing shop-msg lost its volume / restarted without
persistent storage. Two candidate causes:

  (a) Postgres container restart without a persistent volume mount.
      Whatever orchestration brings the messages DB up may be using
      an ephemeral / tmpfs-backed data dir, so any restart wipes
      state.

  (b) Devcontainer attached to a *different* postgres instance than
      the one it had been using. Commit 4949706 ('chore: attach lead
      devcontainer to shopsystem docker network') changed the
      devcontainer's network topology recently and may have
      inadvertently shifted DSN resolution to a different service.

To distinguish:
  - Inspect 'docker volume ls' / 'docker inspect' on the postgres
    service the lead devcontainer's SHOPMSG_DSN points to.
```
</details></tool-use>

    respond             write a BC response message

    read                read a message from a BC's mailboxes
    pending             enumerate pending mailbox entries (queries, not gates)

    prime               session-start orientation: DSN, DB health, pending

    dump                operator debugging: dump messages table rows to stdout

    watch               Monitor-compatible watcher: drain pending messages
                        then LISTEN for new ones, printing one '<work_id>
                        <message_type>' line per event to stdout. Never exits
                        unless the DB is unreachable. Use --bc <name> for
                        inbox watching (BC mode) or --lead <name> for outbox

```text
 tests/conftest.py | 2245 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 2211 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)
---
diff --git a/tests/conftest.py b/tests/conftest.py
index d59e3af..7a8fa67 100644
--- a/tests/conftest.py
+++ b/tests/conftest.py
@@ -17,15 +17,30 @@ import subprocess
 from pathlib import Path
 from typing import Any
 
-# Point the test suite at the local dev Postgres cluster (/tmp/pgrun socket,
-# port 5433) rather than the Docker Compose service name.  The compose
-# service is production infra; during development and CI the cluster is
-# managed by the devcontainer init scripts at host=/tmp/pgrun port=5433.
-# This override must be set before any storage module is imported so the
-# module-level _DEFAULT_DSN is bypassed uniformly for all tests.
-os.environ.setdefault(
-    "SHOPMSG_DSN", "host=/tmp/pgrun port=5433 dbname=shopsystem user=vscode"
+# Route the BDD/unit test suite to an ephemeral Postgres database that is
+# disjoint from the production `shopsystem` database the live `shop-msg`
+# CLI talks to (lead-0bw).  Every row written by any fixture, step def, or
+# in-process CLI invocation lands in `shopsystem_test`; the production DB
+# is never touched.  Postgres NOTIFY channels are per-database, so a
+# `shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product` process attached to the
+# production DSN sees zero NOTIFY events fired by this test session.
+#
+# The override is unconditional (not setdefault) so a developer or CI
+# environment with a stale SHOPMSG_DSN pointing at production cannot
+# silently contaminate the production DB by running pytest.  The DSN's
+# connection parameters mirror the production DSN; only `dbname` differs.
+#
+# Database lifecycle: pytest_sessionstart (below) drops and recreates
+# `shopsystem_test` for a clean slate every session; pytest_sessionfinish
+# drops it again so artefacts never persist across sessions.  The schema
+# (messages, shop_registry, consumed column) is auto-created on first
+# `_connect()` via the existing `_ensure_schema` path.
+SHOPMSG_TEST_DBNAME = "shopsystem_test"
+SHOPMSG_TEST_DSN = (
+    f"postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/{SHOPMSG_TEST_DBNAME}"
 )
+SHOPMSG_MAINT_DSN = "postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/postgres"
+os.environ["SHOPMSG_DSN"] = SHOPMSG_TEST_DSN
 
 import psycopg
 import pytest
@@ -64,9 +79,79 @@ from shop_msg.storage import (
     read_outbox_messages,
     registry_add,
     registry_remove,
+    resolve_shop_name,
 )
 
 
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Ephemeral test database lifecycle (lead-0bw)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# The module-top env override points SHOPMSG_DSN at `shopsystem_test`.
+# These hooks make that database exist before any test runs and drop it
+# after the session completes.  Using DROP/CREATE rather than truncating
+# tables is cheap on the dev cluster and gives a clean slate every session
+# (no schema drift across runs, no leftover NOTIFY channel listeners).
+
+def _admin_execute(sql: str) -> None:
+    """Run a maintenance SQL statement against the cluster's `postgres` DB.
+
+    We connect autocommit=True because CREATE/DROP DATABASE cannot run inside
+    a transaction block.  The maintenance DSN reuses the production cluster
+    credentials but talks to the `postgres` maintenance database; it never
+    touches `shopsystem` (production) or `shopsystem_test` (the ephemeral
+    test DB) directly except via the CREATE/DROP statements issued here.
+    """
+    with psycopg.connect(SHOPMSG_MAINT_DSN, autocommit=True) as conn:
+        with conn.cursor() as cur:
+            cur.execute(sql)
+
+
+def _force_drop_test_database() -> None:
+    """Drop the ephemeral test DB, terminating any lingering connections.
+
+    Pytest sometimes leaves connections open across teardown (e.g. when a
+    background watch process is killed but its connection has not yet been
+    reaped).  DROP DATABASE fails if other backends are connected; the
```

**What the BC delivered (per work_done payload):**

- `SHOPMSG_DSN` unconditionally overridden in `conftest.py` (no longer `setdefault`).
- Claimed empirical verification of all four acceptance criteria: zero NOTIFY events on production DSN during BDD run, zero new rows in production messages/shop_registry, zero pending inbox rows for the lead, zero tmp_path shop_root entries.
- Surfaced four pre-existing BDD failures as unrelated (reproduce on HEAD).
- `scenario_hashes: []` — correct, this was `request_maintenance` with no scenarios.

**Reconciliation outcome: lead-0bw NOT CLOSED (REOPENED).**

The implementation IS present in the BC working tree (`/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging/tests/conftest.py`, +2211/-34 with the matching `SHOPMSG_TEST_DBNAME`/`pytest_sessionstart`/`_force_drop_test_database` content), but it is UNCOMMITTED. `git log origin/main` and `git fetch origin` confirm no lead-0bw commit landed. The BC violated session-close discipline by emitting `work_done` before `git push`.

**Cross-link evidence (commit 8aafbfa, lead-6nt / lead-tsj):** lead-6nt is already CLOSED (it addressed the registry-row save/restore surface separately, before lead-0bw was dispatched). lead-tsj remains OPEN and is NOT resolved by lead-0bw — it's a separate failure mode (postgres LISTEN connection drop / state loss), not a fixture-pollution issue. Existing 16 orphan inbox rows + 333 fxhyg/test-bc/test-lead registry entries from prior un-isolated runs are also NOT cleaned by lead-0bw (the fix only prevents future pollution; lead-497 already tracks the synthetic-row cleanup gap).

**Follow-up beads filed:**
- `lead-b6x` (P1) — lead-0bw reconciliation blocked on BC uncommitted state. Added as dependency: `bd dep add lead-0bw lead-b6x`.
- `lead-9r4` (P2) — request_bugfix for shopsystem-bc-launcher `assert_docker_run_includes_flag` not asserting `container_name` against `run_cmd`. Filed per memory `feedback_*` note now that lead-90z and lead-cyo are both CLOSED. Filed only — not dispatched, as instructed.

**Pending inbox state after consumption attempt:**

- `shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product` is empty.

_**User (2026-05-27 19:47:32Z)**_

Do I need to delete a PAT or log out all clients?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-27 19:47:50Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-27 19:47:57Z)**_

Probably neither — GitHub's secret-scanning label was specifically **"GitHub OAuth Access Token"**, which is the `gho_*` family (issued by an OAuth App during a login flow), not a PAT (`ghp_*`).

What that means:
- **Not a PAT** — nothing to delete in *Personal access tokens*.
- **Not "all clients"** — only one OAuth App authorization issued this token. Revoke that one and any other tokens it issued get invalidated too.

To revoke:
1. GitHub → Settings → **Applications** → **Authorized OAuth Apps** (https://github.com/settings/applications).
2. Find the app that matches the client whose session was being transcribed on 2026-05-22 — likely candidates are `GitHub CLI`, `Claude Code`, or whatever you `gh auth login`'d as.
3. Click **Revoke**. The next use of that client will re-prompt for auth.

If you're unsure which app issued it and want to be safe, revoking *all* OAuth App authorizations is cheap — each affected client just re-auths next time. PATs and SSH keys are unaffected.

One verification you can do: `grep -on '\(gho_\|ghp_\|ghs_\|ghu_\)[A-Za-z0-9_]*' .specstory/history/2026-05-22_17-30-21Z-read-your-instructions.md` if that file still exists in your working tree — the prefix tells you the exact token class.

Practical next steps to identify the token class:

1. **GitHub's security email / notifications** — when secret-scanning fired, GitHub emailed you naming the token type. Check your inbox for a "Secret detected in repository dstengle/shopsystem-product" alert.

3. **GitHub Settings → Security log** (https://github.com/settings/security-log) — filter to 2026-05-22; you'll see exactly which OAuth App or PAT was created/used.

If you'd rather not investigate: revoking all entries in **Authorized OAuth Apps** is cheap (each affected client just re-auths on next use), and leaves PATs / SSH keys untouched. That's the brute-force-safe option.

usage: shop-msg [-h]
                {respond,send,read,pending,consume,prime,dump,watch,registry} ...

positional arguments:
  {respond,send,read,pending,consume,prime,dump,watch,registry}
    respond             write a BC response message
    send                write a lead-to-BC message into a BC's inbox
    read                read a message from a BC's mailboxes
    pending             enumerate pending mailbox entries (queries, not gates)
    consume             mark a mailbox entry as consumed (lead side)
    prime               session-start orientation: DSN, DB health, pending

    dump                operator debugging: dump messages table rows to stdout

    watch               Monitor-compatible watcher: drain pending messages
                        then LISTEN for new ones, printing one '<work_id>
                        <message_type>' line per event to stdout. Never exits
                        unless the DB is unreachable. Use --bc <name> for
                        inbox watching (BC mode) or --lead <name> for outbox
                        watching across all BCs (lead mode).
    registry            manage the shop name registry (name-based addressing)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
```

6	---
7	**Pattern:** the lead shop installs the three framework CLIs (`shop-msg`, `scenarios`, `shop-templates`) by depending on the three BC packages, but has no Python module of its own. Setuptools auto-discovery fails on this layout. Fix in `pyproject.toml`:
8	
9	```toml
10	[tool.setuptools]
11	packages = []
12	```
13	
14	**Apply when:** authoring or editing `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/pyproject.toml`. Don't add a placeholder Python package just to satisfy setuptools — the `packages = []` declaration is the honest answer.
15	
16	**Lead-shop install workflow:**
17	
18	```
19	cd /workspaces/shopsystem-product
20	python -m venv .venv
21	. .venv/bin/activate
22	pip install -e .
23	```
24	
25	After this, `shop-msg`, `scenarios`, `shop-templates` are on PATH. To switch to local editable installs of the BC packages (for cross-repo dev), run `pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/<bc>` per package; this overrides the git+ URL with the local clone.
26	
27	**Watch out:** `pip install --force-reinstall -e <multiple>` mixed with git+ URLs hits ResolutionImpossible. Install BC packages one at a time when reinstalling editable.
28
```
